title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, ESM, Dependencies, and Clean Reproduction · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 plus the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface, ESM semantics, package manager, and platform matrix.
Pin Express 5.2.1, pg 8.22.0, fast-xml-parser 5.10.0, Zod 4.4.3, Pino 10.3.1, PostgreSQL 18.4, and migration tooling from reviewed sources.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Runtime Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 4731 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, TypeScript, dependency, emitted-package, and clean-reproduction evidence; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Ts Clean Reproduction
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: feed-ts-clean-reproduction-1nv96z" that uses case 2071 to reconcile Node, TypeScript, dependency, emitted-package, and clean-reproduction evidence. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, leave timers, handles, or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface, Node 24.18.0, Express 5.2.1, pg 8.22.0, fast-xml-parser 5.10.0, Zod 4.4.3, PostgreSQL 18.4, original unknown fixtures, controlled Fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, disposable databases, handle inspection, package and image review, accessibility, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
